untranslatable_reason: Pattern-based signal (Partial Rise or Decline) requires visual/structural pattern recognition that cannot be expressed through available price action and technical indicators alone. The core logic depends on identifying specific chart pattern formations and their directional prediction capability, which are author-silent on quantitative parameters (success rates, exact threshold levels, volume trends, throwback percentages, and timing metrics are all marked AUTHOR_SILENT). Substitute conditions provided using pivot-based breakout logic as a proxy, but this does not capture the true pattern semantics.
translation_notes: Signal references a named pattern (Partial Rise/Decline) from Bulkowski's work that requires visual pattern identification. Entry approximated as price crossing pivot levels in either direction (CONTEXT_DEPENDENT). Exit conditions set to reversal of recent extremes. Multiple key parameters marked AUTHOR_SILENT including success rates, target move percentages, volume behavior, and stop/target placement rules. This requires manual pattern recognition in live trading.
